engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru DEX Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the K3-VE engine fitted to the Subaru DEX in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data for grey import specialists.

cards.altText: Subaru DEX K3-VE engine - petrol 1.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing belt failure - interference engine design risks valve damage if belt snaps after 60k miles.
VVT solenoid wear - causes rough idle and warning lights on higher-mileage grey imports.
Water pump leakage - common on pre-2010 units, leads to overheating if ignored.

cards.altText: Subaru DEX K3-VE FWD engine - petrol 1.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Ignition coil failure - misfires and rough running reported on UK grey imports after 70k miles.
EGR valve clogging - causes hesitation and emissions test failures on short-journey vehicles.

cards.altText: Subaru DEX K3-VE AWD engine - petrol 1.3L replacement UK
knownFailures: Transfer case fluid degradation - AWD-specific issue causing drivetrain noise if service intervals missed.
Differential mount wear - common on high-mileage AWD imports, leads to clunking on acceleration.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £1,850-£2,100 (supply only) Estimated from comparable K3-VE units - actual quotes may vary.

commonProblems.h2: Subaru DEX Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru DEX relies solely on the K3-VE 1.3L petrol engine across all UK grey import variants. While generally reliable, timing belt failure, VVT solenoid wear and water pump leaks dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Subaru DEX K3-VE Engines Fail Catastrophically
problems.affectedModels: Subaru DEX 1.3i S, 1.3i L, 1.3i AWD (2008-2012, K3-VE)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles if belt not replaced per schedule
problems.rootCause: The K3-VE is an interference engine; a snapped timing belt causes pistons to strike valves, resulting in catastrophic internal damage requiring full replacement.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, water pump before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic damage; recommended every 60k miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Restores vehicle to reliable condition; only option after belt failure

problems.recommendation: On a Subaru DEX worth £3,000-£6,000, preventative belt replacement at £320-£550 offers exceptional value. If the belt has already failed, a rebuilt K3-VE unit at £1,650-£2,100 is the only viable solution - attempting valve repair rarely makes economic sense.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K3-VE engine prices for your Subaru DEX

problems.h4: VVT Solenoid Failure - Rough Idle & Warning Lights on Subaru DEX
problems.affectedModels: Subaru DEX 1.3i S, 1.3i L, 1.3i AWD (2008-2012, K3-VE)
problems.rootCause: Oil sludge buildup or electrical wear in the VVT solenoid disrupts variable valve timing, triggering check engine lights and rough running.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace VVT solenoid, clear ECU codes, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Restores smooth idle; effective if caught before secondary damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded solenoid and adaptation service
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if solenoid failure has caused camshaft or timing damage

problems.recommendation: If your DEX shows only VVT warning lights with no knocking or loss of power, solenoid replacement at £180-£320 is the sensible first step. However, on high-mileage grey imports, upgrading to a rebuilt K3-VE with fresh components often proves better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for VVT-compatible Subaru DEX engine replacement

problems.h4: Water Pump Leakage - Overheating Risk on Subaru DEX K3-VE
problems.affectedModels: Subaru DEX 1.3i S, 1.3i L, 1.3i AWD (2008-2012, K3-VE)
problems.rootCause: Seal degradation in the water pump allows coolant leakage; ignored, this leads to overheating and potential head gasket failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace water pump, thermostat, coolant flush, pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Restores cooling system; recommended during timing belt service

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new pump, revised gaskets, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary if overheating has caused head warpage or gasket failure

problems.recommendation: Water pump replacement at £240-£420 is cost-effective preventative maintenance. If your DEX has already overheated, have a compression test performed - if results are poor, a rebuilt K3-VE at £1,650-£2,100 avoids repeated repair costs.
problems.cta: Find Subaru DEX cooling system compatible engines

problems.h4: Emissions Test Failure - MOT Compliance on Subaru DEX Grey Imports
problems.affectedModels: Subaru DEX all variants (2008-2012, K3-VE grey imports)
problems.typicalFailureMileage: Any mileage if catalytic converter or O2 sensors degraded
problems.rootCause: Age, poor maintenance, or non-UK spec components cause catalytic converter inefficiency, failing MOT emissions tests.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit UK-spec catalytic converter, replace O2 sensors, ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Restores MOT compliance if engine internals are sound

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with UK-spec exhaust components and emissions certification
repairOptions.longevity: Guaranteed MOT pass; includes full documentation for DVLA/IVA

problems.recommendation: For grey import DEX owners facing MOT failure, a rebuilt K3-VE with UK-spec catalytic converter and full emissions documentation at £1,650-£2,100 provides peace of mind and avoids repeated testing costs.
problems.cta: Compare MOT-compliant Subaru DEX engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru DEX
finalCta.paragraph: The Subaru DEX's K3-VE engine is robust when maintained, but grey import status and age mean failures can be costly. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand DEX compatibility, IVA documentation, and MOT compliance. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing original failure points and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and get your DEX back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru DEX Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru DEX and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Subaru DEX Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Subaru DEX production timeline, organised so grey import owners can quickly identify their K3-VE variant and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Subaru DEX launched in Japan with CBA chassis codes, introducing the K3-VE 1.3L petrol engine across FWD and AWD variants.
keyChanges: First production year for Subaru DEX grey imports to UK
CBA-M401F (FWD) and CBA-M411F (AWD) chassis codes introduced
K3-VE engine calibrated to pre-facelift Japanese emissions standards
knownFor: Early production units may require emissions calibration verification for UK MOT
Pre-facelift K3-VE calibration differs from 2010+ DBA chassis units
Strong donor parts availability via shared Daihatsu Materia/Toyota bB platform
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008 Subaru DEX replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru DEX 2008 engine replacement • CBA-M411F AWD compatibility • K3-VE pre-facelift calibration

years.description: The 2010 facelift introduced DBA chassis codes with updated K3-VE emissions calibration, essential for UK MOT compliance on grey imports.
keyChanges: DBA-M401F (FWD) and DBA-M411F (AWD) chassis codes replaced CBA variants
Updated K3-VE emissions calibration for stricter Japanese standards
Final production year 2012 marked end of DEX availability for UK grey imports
mainEngines: K3-VE - 1.3L petrol (1.3i S, 1.3i L, 1.3i AWD) with revised calibration
knownFor: Updated emissions calibration simplifies UK MOT and IVA test compliance
DBA chassis units command premium pricing due to compliance advantages
Final production year 2012 units are rarest and most sought-after by collectors
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010 Subaru DEX replacement engines
years.ticker: Subaru DEX 2010 engine replacement • DBA-M411F AWD compatibility • K3-VE Euro 4 compliance verification

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Subaru DEX was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Subaru DEX specialists.

faq.h2: Subaru DEX Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru DEX engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru DEX engine replacement costs range from £895 for Japanese used imports to £2,100 for UK reconditioned units, depending on condition, variant and supplier. Supply & fit services add £450-£850 labour.
keyPoints: Japanese used K3-VE engines start from £895 with auction verification
UK reconditioned units range £1,650-£2,100 with 12-month warranty
AWD variants (DBA-M411F) typically cost £200-£300 more than FWD
Supply & fit labour adds £450-£850 depending on specialist location
items.cta: Compare Subaru DEX engine replacement quotes from vetted UK specialists

items.question: What engine code fits my Subaru DEX?
items.answer: All Subaru DEX variants use the K3-VE 1.3L petrol engine code, but chassis codes DBA-M401F (FWD) and DBA-M411F (AWD) determine compatibility with your vehicle's drivetrain.
keyPoints: K3-VE is the sole engine code across all DEX variants 2008-2012
Verify chassis code DBA-M401F for FWD or DBA-M411F for AWD compatibility
Enter your registration above for instant DVLA-verified engine code confirmation
K3-VE was also fitted to Daihatsu Materia and Toyota bB for wider parts availability
items.cta: Verify your Subaru DEX K3-VE engine code instantly via DVLA lookup

items.question: Is a reconditioned Subaru DEX engine better than a used Japanese import?
items.answer: Reconditioned K3-VE engines offer UK warranty coverage and upgraded components addressing known failures, while used Japanese imports provide lower upfront cost but limited warranty and potential compliance documentation gaps.
keyPoints: Reconditioned units include 12-month UK warranty vs 3-6 months for imports
Rebuilt engines feature upgraded timing components addressing K3-VE failure patterns
Japanese imports require additional IVA/MOT documentation verification for UK compliance
Budget-conscious owners may prefer imports; long-term owners benefit from reconditioned reliability
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs used Subaru DEX engine options with warranty details

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my Subaru DEX UK MOT or registration?
items.answer: Replacement engines must meet UK emissions standards and include proper documentation for DVLA registration; EngineMarket specialists supply K3-VE units with Euro 4 certification and IVA support.
keyPoints: All replacement K3-VE engines include UK-spec catalytic converter for MOT compliance

items.question: How long does Subaru DEX engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: UK reconditioned K3-VE engines typically dispatch within 2 days; Japanese imports require 10-14 days delivery plus 1 day fitting. Supply & fit bookings can often be scheduled within 48 hours for in-stock units.

Japanese import engines: 10-14 days delivery plus 1 day specialist fitting

items.question: Can I fit a Subaru DEX engine myself to save money?
items.answer: DIY engine fitting is not recommended for Subaru DEX grey imports due to ECU immobiliser adaptation, AWD drivetrain calibration and UK compliance documentation requirements best handled by specialists.
keyPoints: K3-VE ECU requires immobiliser adaptation for UK registration - specialist tooling needed
AWD variants need transfer case fluid specification and differential calibration
IVA/MOT documentation support requires specialist knowledge of grey import compliance
Supply & fit packages often cost less than DIY when factoring tool hire and risk of errors

items.question: What are the most common Subaru DEX engine problems?
items.answer: Timing belt failure on the interference-design K3-VE, VVT solenoid wear causing rough idle, and water pump leakage on pre-2010 units dominate Subaru DEX replacement enquiries from UK grey import owners.
keyPoints: Timing belt must be replaced every 60k miles to prevent catastrophic valve damage
VVT solenoid failure triggers check engine lights and requires ECU adaptation service
Water pump leakage common on pre-2010 units; replace during timing belt service
All rebuilt K3-VE units include upgraded components addressing these known failure points
items.cta: Compare Subaru DEX engine replacements with failure-proof upgraded components

items.question: Is it worth repairing or replacing my Subaru DEX engine?
items.answer: If repair costs exceed 60% of a reconditioned K3-VE price (£1,650-£2,100), replacement offers better long-term value with UK warranty coverage and upgraded components addressing original failure patterns.
keyPoints: Minor repairs (solenoid, water pump) cost £180-£420 and may be viable for low-mileage units
Major failures (timing belt snap, overheating damage) typically require full replacement
Rebuilt K3-VE engines include 12-month warranty vs limited coverage on repaired units
Consider vehicle value: DEX grey imports typically worth £3,000-£6,000 in UK market
items.cta: Get instant repair vs replace cost comparison for your Subaru DEX variant

items.question: Does the Subaru DEX engine work with Daihatsu or Toyota donor parts?
items.answer: Yes - the K3-VE engine was also fitted to Daihatsu Materia and Toyota bB/Scion xB, expanding UK parts availability and reducing lead times for grey import DEX owners seeking replacement components.
keyPoints: K3-VE cross-compatibility increases donor pool for hard-to-find DEX components
Timing kits, gaskets and sensors often available from Daihatsu/Toyota suppliers

EngineMarket specialists source from cross-brand inventory to reduce wait times
